title: verbascum lanatum
intro: Verbascum lanatum, also known as woolly mullein, is a biennial or perennial plant species native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa, characterized by its tall stems and large, fuzzy leaves. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as coughs, colds, and bronchitis, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Verbascum lanatum include soothing and calming the nervous system, reducing inflammation, and promoting a good night's sleep. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, as well as being used topically to soothe skin irritations and wounds.
